name: Micro Wake Word
description: ESPHome implements an on-device wake word detection framework from [microWakeWord](https://github.com/kahrendt/microWakeWord). This repository/library allows you to create a custom wake word for your ESPHome device.

config_entries.label: Models
config_entries.description: The models to use. Only the first model is enabled by default on the first boot. Each model's enabled state is then saved/restored to/from the flash.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The optional ID used for the model actions below.

config_entries.label: Microphone
config_entries.description: The [microphone](https://esphome.io/components/microphone/) settings to use for audio input.

config_entries.label: Stop After Detection
config_entries.description: Whether to stop the component after detecting a wake word. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Vad
config_entries.description: Enable a Voice Activity Detection model to reduce false accepts from non-speech sounds. e.g. github://esphome/micro-wake-word-models/models/v2/vad.json@main. e.g. https://github.com/esphome/micro-wake-word-models/raw/main/models/v2/vad.json. e.g. /config/models/vad.json. If the probability is below this value, then no wake word will be accepted. A larger value reduces the number of false accepts but increases the number of false rejections. The probability_cutoff and sliding_window_size are provided by the JSON file but can be overridden in YAML. A default VAD model is provided with the vad configuration variables, but a different model can be overridden in YAML.
